ddls:
CREATE TABLE "server_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "host" INTEGER /* ID of the host making the request */,
  "method" INTEGER /* ID of the HTTP method used for the request */,
  "endpoint" INTEGER /* ID of the URL endpoint accessed */,
  "protocol" INTEGER /* ID of the protocol used for the request */,
  "status" INTEGER /* ID of the HTTP status code returned */,
  "content_size" INTEGER /* Size of the content returned in bytes */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the request */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* Information about the client software (e.g., browser, version) */,
  "referrer" TEXT /* URL of the referring page */,
  "response_time" INTEGER /* Time taken to process the request in milliseconds */,
  "ssl_flag" INTEGER /* Indicates if the connection was secure (HTTPS) */,
  "geolocation_id" INTEGER /* ID of the geolocation data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_server_logs_host FOREIGN KEY ("host") REFERENCES hosts ("host_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_server_logs_endpoint FOREIGN KEY ("endpoint") REFERENCES endpoints ("endpoint_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_server_logs_status FOREIGN KEY ("status") REFERENCES statuses ("status_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_server_logs_protocol FOREIGN KEY ("protocol") REFERENCES protocols ("protocol_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_server_logs_method FOREIGN KEY ("method") REFERENCES methods ("method_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_server_logs_geolocation_id FOREIGN KEY ("geolocation_id") REFERENCES geolocations ("geolocation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "hosts" (
  "host_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each host */,
  "host_name" TEXT /* Hostname or IP address of the client */,
  "host_type" TEXT /* Type of the host (e.g., server, desktop, mobile) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the host */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country of the host */,
  "city" TEXT /* City of the host */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("host_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "methods" (
  "method_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each HTTP method */,
  "method_name" TEXT /* Name of the HTTP method (e.g., GET, POST, PUT) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("method_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "endpoints" (
  "endpoint_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each endpoint */,
  "endpoint_path" TEXT /* URL path of the endpoint */,
  "endpoint_description" TEXT /* Description of the endpoint */,
  "category_id" INTEGER /* ID of the category the endpoint belongs to */,
  "access_count" INTEGER /* Number of times the endpoint has been accessed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("endpoint_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_endpoints_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("category_id") REFERENCES categories ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "protocols" (
  "protocol_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each protocol */,
  "protocol_name" TEXT /* Name of the protocol (e.g., HTTP/1.0, HTTP/1.1) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("protocol_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "statuses" (
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each status code */,
  "status_code" INTEGER /* HTTP status code */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the status code */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., analyst, administrator) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for secure authentication */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login timestamp */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user account is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "log_id" INTEGER /* ID of the log entry being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the log entry */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the log entry was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* Information about the client software (e.g., browser, version) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_log_id FOREIGN KEY ("log_id") REFERENCES server_logs ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "log_id" INTEGER /* ID of the log entry that triggered the alert */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., security, performance) */,
  "alert_message" TEXT /* Message describing the alert */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alert was triggered */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the alert (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "resolved" INTEGER /* Indicates if the alert has been resolved */,
  "resolution_notes" TEXT /* Notes on the resolution of the alert */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_log_id FOREIGN KEY ("log_id") REFERENCES server_logs ("log_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "geolocations" (
  "geolocation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each geolocation */,
  "latitude" TEXT /* Latitude of the location */,
  "longitude" TEXT /* Longitude of the location */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country of the location */,
  "city" TEXT /* City of the location */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("geolocation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the category (e.g., static content, API, dynamic content) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the category */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)
